title: NOC 14400 – Shippers and Receivers | GTR Immigration
description: Shippers and Receivers play a vital role in supply chains by coordinating inbound and outbound freight, managing warehouse documentation, and ensuring smooth goods movement across retail, manufacturing, and industrial sectors in Canada. Classified under NOC 14400, this occupation has experienced strong post-pandemic hiring growth and is expected to remain in steady demand—creating realistic immigration pathways through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.teer: Growth in e-commerce and logistics networksExpansion of manufacturing outputEntry-level TEER occupation with advancement potentialEmployer-supported recruitmentSupply-chain modernization
classification.major_group: 144 – Supply chain logistics, tracking, and scheduling coordination occupations
classification.minor_group: 1440 – Supply chain logistics, tracking, and scheduling coordination occupations

duties: Shippers and Receivers may perform some or all of the following:
Arrange shipment methods and prepare bills of lading, customs forms, invoices, and shipping documents
Assemble containers, pack goods, and label shipments
Inspect inbound goods and record shortages or damage
Maintain manual or computerized inventory systems
employment_requirements: Experience in warehouse or logistics roles (often required)
Valid driver’s licence for some positions
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create approximately 39,600 new positions for Shippers and Receivers, while around 35,500 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and targeted immigration planning especially valuable for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Shippers and Receivers are in demand across retail, wholesale, and manufacturing operations nationwide. Hiring has expanded rapidly since the pandemic and is projected to continue over the coming years.
Retail and wholesale distribution centresManufacturing plantsLogistics warehousesPublic-sector supply depotsIndustrial facilities
Annual salaries typically range between $28,700 and $54,900 CAD, depending on province, experience, and certifications.
